DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Scher, Esq. (415-788-4545) of Wykowski for Plaintiff.
Nicholas Costanza, Esq. (415-392-1960) of Sideman Bancroft LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant contests - complaint contains general deficiencies; Plaintiff contests conversion, fiduciary duty and fraudulent concealment. Plaintiff argues specificity. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAMON SLAGLE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

The demurrers are all OVERRULED. The complaint's allegations are sufficiently certain and unambiguous. The complaint properly alleges conversion of an ascertainable amount of money that can be determined in discovery. (Haigler v. Donnelly (1941) 18 Cal.2d 674, 681.) The alleged discretionary duties in defendant's managerial job give rise to a fiduciary duty. (Daniel Orifice Fitting Co. v. Whalen (1962) 198 Cal.App.2d 791.) The facts of fraudulent concealment are sufficiently pled, given defendant's superior access to information. (Casino v. Bank of America (2014) 22 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1469.) (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, email:rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)
